region_of_origin: United States (widely adopted globally, including Singapore)
food_subgroup: Baked or chilled cheesecake with fruit topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general cheesecake entry (FOODON_03301116); no specific strawberry cheesecake variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers cheesecake as a category; no strawberry-specific variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for strawberry cheesecake identified in the clinical terminology. Ontology gap noted.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of strawberry cheesecake weighing approximately 125 g, as typically served in a Singapore cafe or restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 18139)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for cheesecake prepared from recipe used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values are representative of a classic cream-cheese cheesecake with biscuit crust. Strawberry topping/swirl adds minor sugar contribution; per-100g values adjusted upward slightly for sugar_g to reflect strawberry component, consistent with USDA Branded Food entries for strawberry cheesecake variants. All core macros sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 18139.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake GI estimate — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous to high-fat, high-sugar baked dessert category
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for strawberry cheesecake exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Cheesecake is a high-fat, moderate-carbohydrate dessert. Fat and protein content significantly blunt glycemic response. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogy with similar high-fat baked desserts (e.g., chocolate cake GI ~38, pound cake GI ~38) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load. GL per serving = (35 x 26.0 x 1.25) / 100 = 11.4.

hypertension.guidance: One slice of strawberry cheesecake provides approximately 275 mg sodium and 15 g saturated fat; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and frequency of consumption, as regular intake of high-sodium, high-saturated-fat desserts may contribute to elevated blood pressure and arterial stiffness.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar and refined carbohydrate content despite low GI
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index of cheesecake is low (approximately 35) due to its high fat content blunting glucose absorption, one slice delivers approximately 23 g of sugar and 32.5 g total carbohydrate, which can still raise postprandial blood glucose in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; consumption should be occasional and portion-controlled, with blood glucose monitoring advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat and cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: One slice of strawberry cheesecake contains approximately 15 g saturated fat and 131 mg cholesterol, both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease or dyslipidaemia should treat this as an occasional indulgence and prioritise heart-healthy dietary patterns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Strawberry Cheesecake (1 Slice) is a rich, creamy dessert consisting of a dense cream-cheese filling set on a buttery biscuit or graham-cracker crust, topped or swirled with strawberry jam, fresh strawberries, or strawberry coulis. It may be baked (New York style) or chilled without baking (no-bake style). The texture is smooth, dense, and velvety, with a sweet-tart contrast from the strawberry component. In Singapore, cheesecake slices are widely available in cafes, dessert shops, and casual dining restaurants such as The Noodle Place. Nutritionally, one 125 g slice provides approximately 401 kcal, 6.9 g protein, 28.1 g fat (15 g saturated), and 32.5 g carbohydrate including 23 g sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low) due to the high fat content slowing glucose absorption, but the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 11.4. The high saturated fat and cholesterol content make this a high-concern food for cardiovascular disease. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the sugar load warrants portion control despite the low GI. Sodium content is moderate at approximately 275 mg per slice. This dessert is vegetarian but not vegan, contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, and is not suitable for those with lactose intolerance or egg allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of strawberry cheesecake?
Is strawberry cheesecake suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of cheesecake?
How much fat is in a cheesecake slice?
Can I eat cheesecake if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC 18139 (cheesecake prepared from recipe), which is an approved secondary global source. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry for strawberry cheesecake was identified. GI is an analogous estimate based on comparable high-fat baked desserts from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match in a primary regional source was found.
notes: Serving size of 125 g is an estimate for a standard restaurant slice; actual weight may vary by establishment. The Noodle Place (Circular Road) is the sole field survey source restaurant for this record; no independent nutritional disclosure was available from the restaurant. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC 18139 and are representative of a classic cream-cheese cheesecake; the strawberry component adds minor sugar. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are reported from USDA SR Legacy FDC 18139; values are numeric and sourced. No sentinel -1 values are used without source justification — all micronutrient values above are drawn from the USDA source entry. GI estimated at 35 (low) by analogy with high-fat baked desserts per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). GL per serving = (35 x 26.0 x 1.25) / 100 = 11.4. Halal status is unconfirmed as the restaurant has not published halal certification; is_halal set to false by default. Kosher status unconfirmed; is_kosher set to false.
